name: Firewall Configuration with nftables
description: Configure and manage firewall rules using nftables or ufw to control network traffic and protect services.

steps.title: Check current firewall status
steps.description: View the current firewall status and rules.
steps.command: sudo ufw status verbose 2>/dev/null || sudo nft list ruleset 2>/dev/null || sudo iptables -L -n

steps.title: Enable ufw and set default policies
steps.description: Enable the firewall with deny-by-default for incoming traffic.
steps.command: sudo ufw default deny incoming && sudo ufw default allow outgoing && sudo ufw enable
steps.warning: Make sure to allow SSH before enabling the firewall or you may lock yourself out.

steps.title: Allow specific services
steps.description: Open ports for services that need to accept incoming connections.
steps.command: sudo ufw allow ssh && sudo ufw allow 80/tcp && sudo ufw allow 443/tcp
steps.tip: You can also use service names: sudo ufw allow 'Nginx Full'

steps.title: Allow access from a specific IP
steps.description: Restrict access to a port from a specific IP address or subnet.
steps.command: sudo ufw allow from 10.0.0.0/24 to any port 5432 proto tcp
steps.tip: This is useful for database ports that should only be accessible from your application servers.

steps.title: View and delete specific rules
steps.description: List numbered rules and delete a specific one.
steps.command: sudo ufw status numbered && echo 'Delete with: sudo ufw delete <number>'

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Firewall Configuration
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Firewall Status ==="
if command -v ufw &>/dev/null; then
  sudo ufw status verbose
elif command -v nft &>/dev/null; then
  sudo nft list ruleset | head -30
else
  sudo iptables -L -n | head -20
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Open Ports ==="
ss -tlnp

echo ""
echo "=== Firewall Commands (ufw) ==="
echo "Enable:       sudo ufw enable"
echo "Disable:      sudo ufw disable"
echo "Allow port:   sudo ufw allow 80/tcp"
echo "Allow SSH:    sudo ufw allow ssh"
echo "Allow IP:     sudo ufw allow from 10.0.0.0/24"
echo "Deny:         sudo ufw deny 3306/tcp"
echo "Delete:       sudo ufw delete allow 80/tcp"
echo "Reset:        sudo ufw reset"

faq.question: Should I use ufw or nftables directly?
faq.answer: ufw is a user-friendly frontend for iptables/nftables. Use it for simple server setups. Use nftables directly for complex rules, performance tuning, or when you need features like sets and maps.

faq.question: How do I prevent locking myself out?
faq.answer: Always allow SSH before enabling the firewall: 'sudo ufw allow ssh'. If using a cloud provider, also ensure the security group/network ACL allows SSH access.
